tomato_early_blight.symptoms: Dark brown spots with concentric rings, usually starting on older, lower leaves.
organicTreatment: Remove and destroy affected leaves immediately — don't compost them
Spray a neem oil solution every 7-10 days on remaining foliage
Apply a baking soda spray (1 tbsp baking soda + a few drops of liquid soap per liter of water) as a mild fungicide
Improve airflow by pruning lower leaves that touch the soil
prevention: Rotate crops — avoid planting tomatoes in the same spot each year
Water at the base of the plant, not on the leaves
Mulch around the base to stop soil splashing onto leaves

tomato_late_blight.symptoms: Large, water-soaked dark patches on leaves and stems that spread fast, especially in cool, wet weather.
organicTreatment: Remove and destroy infected plants promptly — this disease spreads very quickly
Apply a copper-based organic fungicide as soon as symptoms appear
Avoid overhead watering and increase spacing between plants for airflow
prevention: Choose blight-resistant tomato varieties where available
Avoid working in the garden when foliage is wet
Space plants generously to reduce humidity around leaves

tomato_healthy.symptoms: No signs of disease detected. Leaves are green and uniform.
organicTreatment: No treatment needed
Continue regular watering and monitoring
prevention: Keep monitoring weekly for early signs of pests or disease
Maintain good soil nutrition with compost

tomato_leaf_mold.symptoms: Pale yellow spots on the upper leaf surface with olive-green to gray fuzzy mold underneath.
organicTreatment: Remove affected leaves and improve ventilation around plants
Reduce humidity — avoid overhead watering, especially late in the day
Apply a sulfur-based organic fungicide if the problem persists
prevention: Space plants well and stake/trellis to improve airflow
Water early in the day so leaves dry before evening

tomato_bacterial_spot.symptoms: Small, dark, water-soaked spots on leaves and fruit that may develop yellow halos.
organicTreatment: Remove severely affected leaves and fruit
Apply an organic copper-based bactericide
Avoid handling plants when leaves are wet to prevent spreading bacteria
prevention: Use disease-free seeds and seedlings
Rotate crops and avoid overhead irrigation

potato_early_blight.symptoms: Dark, target-like concentric rings on older leaves, often starting near the base of the plant.
organicTreatment: Remove infected foliage as soon as it's noticed
Apply a copper-based organic fungicide on a 7-10 day cycle
Ensure plants have good spacing for airflow
prevention: Rotate potato crops every 2-3 years
Hill soil around the base to protect tubers
Avoid overhead watering

potato_late_blight.symptoms: Dark, greasy-looking lesions on leaves that spread rapidly, especially in cool, damp weather.
organicTreatment: Remove and destroy infected plants immediately
Apply copper-based organic fungicide preventively before rainy periods
Harvest unaffected tubers early if the disease is spreading in the field
prevention: Plant certified disease-free seed potatoes
Avoid excess irrigation and improve field drainage

organicTreatment: No treatment needed
Continue regular monitoring
prevention: Maintain crop rotation and healthy soil

corn_common_rust.symptoms: Small, reddish-brown, raised pustules scattered on both sides of the leaves.
organicTreatment: Remove heavily infected lower leaves
Apply a sulfur-based organic fungicide if the infection is spreading fast
Ensure adequate spacing between plants
prevention: Plant rust-resistant corn varieties
Avoid dense planting that traps humidity

corn_northern_leaf_blight.symptoms: Long, gray-green, cigar-shaped lesions on the leaves.
organicTreatment: Remove and destroy severely infected leaves
Apply an organic copper-based fungicide early in the infection
Improve field drainage and airflow
prevention: Rotate crops with non-host plants
Choose resistant hybrid varieties where possible

organicTreatment: No treatment needed
Continue regular monitoring
prevention: Maintain good soil nutrition and crop rotation

apple_scab.symptoms: Olive-green to dark scabby spots on leaves and fruit, which can cause leaves to curl and drop early.
organicTreatment: Remove and destroy fallen leaves in autumn to reduce overwintering spores
Apply an organic sulfur or copper-based fungicide starting at bud break
Prune to improve air circulation through the canopy
prevention: Choose scab-resistant apple varieties
Rake and dispose of fallen leaves each season

apple_black_rot.symptoms: Purple-bordered spots on leaves and sunken, rotting lesions on fruit.
organicTreatment: Prune out dead or diseased wood and destroy it
Remove mummified fruit left on the tree or ground
Apply an organic copper-based fungicide during the growing season
prevention: Maintain good tree vigor with proper pruning and nutrition
Remove nearby infected plant debris promptly

organicTreatment: No treatment needed
Continue regular monitoring
prevention: Prune annually and maintain orchard sanitation

pepper_bacterial_spot.symptoms: Small, water-soaked spots on leaves and fruit that turn brown and may cause leaves to drop.
organicTreatment: Remove and destroy affected leaves and fruit
Apply an organic copper-based bactericide
Avoid working with plants while foliage is wet
prevention: Use certified disease-free seeds
Rotate crops and avoid overhead watering

organicTreatment: No treatment needed
Continue regular monitoring
prevention: Maintain crop rotation and balanced soil nutrition

brinjal_leaf_spot.symptoms: Small brown-to-grey spots on leaves, sometimes with a yellow halo, which can merge and cause leaf drop.
organicTreatment: Remove and destroy affected leaves
Spray a neem oil solution every 7-10 days
Apply an organic copper-based fungicide if spreading continues
prevention: Avoid overhead watering
Space plants for good airflow
Rotate crops each season

organicTreatment: No treatment needed
Continue regular monitoring
prevention: Maintain crop rotation and balanced soil nutrition

cabbage_black_rot.symptoms: Yellow, V-shaped lesions starting at leaf edges that turn brown as veins blacken.
organicTreatment: Remove and destroy infected leaves and plant debris
Apply an organic copper-based bactericide
Avoid working with wet plants to prevent spreading
prevention: Use disease-free seeds
Rotate crops for 2-3 years between cabbage-family plantings

organicTreatment: No treatment needed
Continue regular monitoring
prevention: Maintain crop rotation and balanced soil nutrition

cauliflower_black_rot.symptoms: Yellow V-shaped lesions on leaf margins with blackened veins, similar to cabbage black rot.
organicTreatment: Remove and destroy infected leaves and debris
Apply an organic copper-based bactericide
Avoid overhead irrigation
prevention: Use certified disease-free seedlings
Rotate with non-cruciferous crops

organicTreatment: No treatment needed
Continue regular monitoring
prevention: Maintain crop rotation and balanced soil nutrition

peanut_leaf_spot.symptoms: Small, dark brown circular spots on leaves, often with a yellow halo.
organicTreatment: Remove and destroy heavily affected leaves
Apply an organic sulfur or copper-based fungicide
Improve airflow between plants
prevention: Rotate with non-legume crops
Avoid dense planting
Remove crop residue after harvest

organicTreatment: No treatment needed
Continue regular monitoring
prevention: Maintain crop rotation and balanced soil nutrition
